citation.supportingQuote: PD usually presents in later life with the cardinal clinical motor features of bradykinesia, resting tremor, and rigidity, often in various combinations. Postural instability emerges later in the disease course as another defining feature.

questions.stem: Most effective medication for Parkinson disease?

citation.supportingQuote: Oral levodopa is the most effective treatment for the motor symptoms of PD...The diagnosis will likely be wrong if a patient does not have a good motor response to levodopa treatment.

questions.stem: Resting tremor in Parkinson disease characteristically:

citation.supportingQuote: After using the hands, such as to pick up a book, the tremor may subside for some minutes, only to return when the patient is distracted and at rest once again. This is the so-called reemerging tremor that is typical of PD.

questions.stem: Drug-induced parkinsonism differs from PD by being:
questions.correctAnswer: Symmetric, often without resting tremor, and reversible

citation.supportingQuote: An essential part of the evaluation of PD is to exclude the effect of medications that can lead to extrapyramidal side effects and motor manifestations that are, at times, indistinguishable from classical PD. Typically, it is the traditional antipsychotics that are implied. In these cases, the nonmotor manifestations of PD, such as anosmia, are not found.
The common side effects of L-dopa include GI upsets, dyskinesia, and psychosis with hallucinations.

questions.stem: What distinguishes Lewy body dementia from Parkinson disease dementia?
questions.correctAnswer: In LBD, dementia occurs BEFORE or within 1 year of motor symptoms

citation.supportingQuote: Lewy body dementia will have the added features of earlier onset of dementia and hallucinations, especially vivid, formed visual hallucinations. These patients are also susceptible to the extrapyramidal side effects of neuroleptics.

questions.stem: Why should levodopa NEVER be abruptly discontinued?

citation.supportingQuote: Most anti-Parkinsonian medications provide good symptom control for 3 to 6 years.

questions.stem: Impulse control disorders (gambling, hypersexuality) are a side effect of:

citation.supportingQuote: Adverse events associated with elevated dopamine levels reaching the CNS include hallucinations, impulse control disorders, depression, and heightened suicidal tendencies.
